ground_truth_fix: TL;DR. You are missing bug fix 2fa7d94afc1a for the BPF verifier. It was backported to the v5.13 kernel you are using as commit e7a61f15beea, but not to the v5.10 kernel. You might want to try a newer Android kernel if possible, or to ask them to carry the bugfix if they don't on v5.10. The verifier tells us that we are trying to access the packet at offset 14 (off=14) with an access size of 1 byte when the packet is only known to be at least 13 bytes long (r=13). The known packet length here is incorrect somehow because you checked that address >= end.

candidate_repairs.patch_hint: Add an explicit unsigned clamp and keep the offset calculation in a separate verified register
candidate_repairs.rationale: A verifier-visible proof existed earlier, but arithmetic lowering widened the offset before the rejected access.

evidence.detail: invalid access to packet, off=14 size=1, R1(id=2,off=14,r=13)
A verifier-visible proof existed earlier, but arithmetic lowering widened the offset before the rejected access.
Verifier symptom: invalid access to packet, off=14 size=1, R1(id=2,off=14,r=13)
Proof existed earlier and was lost at insn 18 (RANGE_LOSS).

metadata.help: Add an explicit unsigned clamp and keep the offset calculation in a separate verified register
metadata.note: A verifier-visible proof existed earlier, but arithmetic lowering widened the offset before the rejected access.

notes: A verifier-visible proof existed earlier, but arithmetic lowering widened the offset before the rejected access.
invalid access to packet, off=14 size=1, R1(id=2,off=14,r=13)

oblige_json.raw_log_excerpt: invalid access to packet, off=14 size=1, R1(id=2,off=14,r=13)

oblige_output:
error[OBLIGE-E005]: lowering_artifact — packet access with lost proof
  ┌─ <bytecode>
  │
35 │     r1 = r8
   │     ─────── proof established
   │     R1: scalar(umax=50, var_off=(0x0; 0xffffffff)) → scalar(umax=50, var_off=(0x0; 0xffffffff))
   │
35 │     r1 = r8
   │     ─────── proof lost: verifier-visible bounds were lost
   │     R1: scalar(umax=50, var_off=(0x0; 0xffffffff)) → scalar(umax=50, var_off=(0x0; 0xffffffff))
   │
39 │     r3 = *(u8 *)(r1 +0)
   │     ─────────────────── rejected
   │     R1: pkt(range=13, off=14) → pkt(range=0, off=14)
   │
  = note: A verifier-visible proof existed earlier, but arithmetic lowering widened the offset before the rejected access.
  = help: Add an explicit unsigned clamp and keep the offset calculation in a separate verified register
prompt_a:
Here is eBPF code that fails verification. Fix it.

Code:
unsafe fn xdp_test(ctx: XdpContext) -> XdpResult {
let data = ctx.data()?;
let start = ctx.data_start();
let off = data.offset();
let end = ctx.data_end();
/* Ensuring an upper bound for off doesn't make any difference
if off > 50 {
return XdpResult::Err(OutOfBounds);
}
*/
let mut address = start + off;
for i in 0..500 {
address = start + off + i;
if address <= start || address >= end {
break;
}
// This line (packet access) fails on kernel 5.10, but works fine on 5.13
let byte = *(address as *const u8);
// Just so the packet read above doesn't get optimized away
printk!("%u", byte as u32);
}
Ok(XdpAction::Pass)
}
